revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsunozuka Kofun | native_name = 角塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsunozuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsunozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Iwate Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsunozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|39|08|29|N|141|05|37|E||display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōshū, Iwate]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th or early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1935, 1945, 1947, 1974-75 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Tsunozuka Kofun'''|角塚古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the Isawa neighborhood of the city of [[Ōshū, Iwate]] in the [[Tōhoku region]] of northern [[Japan]]. It is significant in that it is the northernmost keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' yet discovered,<ref>{{Cite book|last=Mizoguchi|first=Koji|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=CZM2AQAAQBAJ|title=The Archaeology of Japan: From the Earliest Rice Farming Villages to the Rise of the State|date=|publisher=Cambridge University Press|year=2013|isbn=978-0-521-88490-7|location=|pages=242n|language=en|author-link=Koji Mizoguchi}}</ref> and indicates the penetration of [[Kofun period]] culture into lands traditionally considered part of the [[Emishi]] cultural zone.
==Overview== The Tsunozuka Kofun is located in the southern portion of the {{Interlanguage link|Kitakami Basin|ja|北上盆地}} of the [[Kitakami River]] on the [[alluvial fan]] of the [[Isawa River]]. The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name or rank of the person buried within is unknown. From the style of the mound and [[grave goods]] uncovered within, archaeologists have dated the ''kofun'' to the late 5th or early 6th century AD.
The ''kofun'' was excavated in 1935 and 1945, during which time a number of ''[[haniwa]]'' in the shapes of animals, people and buildings were discovered. However, it was not until 1947 that archaeologists confirmed that the structure was that of a {{nihongo|keyhole-shaped ''kofun''|前方後円墳|zenpō-kōen-fun}}. The site received protection as an Iwate Prefectural Historic Site in 1957. During further excavations held from 1974-1975, more ''haniwa'' and the ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones which originally covered the tomb were uncovered. The site received further protection as a National Historic Site in 1985.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp?register_id=401&item_id=168 |title=角塚古墳|trans-title=Tsunozuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
*Total length: 43–45 meters *Circular Portion: 2-tier, 28 meter diameter x 4 meter height *Keyway Portion: 10 meter width in the north, tapering out to 15 meters in the south, 1.5 meter height
The site is about 15 minutes by car from [[JR East]] [[Tōhoku Main Line]] [[Mizusawa-Esashi Station]].The artifacts uncovered in excavations are preserved at the Isawa Local History Museum.
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Iwate)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Tsunozuka-kofun}} * [http://www.city.oshu.iwate.jp/kanko/view.rbz?nd=401&ik=1&pnp=397&pnp=401&cd=1567 Oshu City Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215759 Cultural Heritage Online] {{in lang|ja}}
[[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Iwate Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōshū, Iwate]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsunozuka Kofun | native_name = 角塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsunozuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsunozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Iwate Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsunozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|39|08|29|N|141|05|37|E||display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōshū, Iwate]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th or early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1935, 1945, 1947, 1974-75 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Tsunozuka Kofun'''|角塚古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the Isawa neighborhood of the city of [[Ōshū, Iwate]] in the [[Tōhoku region]] of northern [[Japan]]. It is significant in that it is the northernmost keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' yet discovered,<ref>{{Cite book|last=Mizoguchi|first=Koji|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=CZM2AQAAQBAJ|title=The Archaeology of Japan: From the Earliest Rice Farming Villages to the Rise of the State|date=|publisher=Cambridge University Press|year=2013|isbn=978-0-521-88490-7|location=|pages=242n|language=en|author-link=Koji Mizoguchi}}</ref> and indicates the penetration of [[Kofun period]] culture into lands traditionally considered part of the [[Emishi]] cultural zone.
==Overview== The Tsunozuka Kofun is located in the southern portion of the {{Interlanguage link|Kitakami Basin|ja|北上盆地}} of the [[Kitakami River]] on the [[alluvial fan]] of the [[Isawa River]]. The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name or rank of the person buried within is unknown. From the style of the mound and [[grave goods]] uncovered within, archaeologists have dated the ''kofun'' to the late 5th or early 6th century AD.
The ''kofun'' was excavated in 1935 and 1945, during which time a number of ''[[haniwa]]'' in the shapes of animals, people and buildings were discovered. However, it was not until 1947 that archaeologists confirmed that the structure was that of a {{nihongo|keyhole-shaped ''kofun''|前方後円墳|zenpō-kōen-fun}}. The site received protection as an Iwate Prefectural Historic Site in 1957. During further excavations held from 1974-1975, more ''haniwa'' and the ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones which originally covered the tomb were uncovered. The site received further protection as a National Historic Site in 1985.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp?register_id=401&item_id=168 |title=角塚古墳|trans-title=Tsunozuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
*Total length: 43–45 meters *Circular Portion: 2-tier, 28 meter diameter x 4 meter height *Keyway Portion: 10 meter width in the north, tapering out to 15 meters in the south, 1.5 meter height
The site is about 15 minutes by car from [[JR East]] [[Tōhoku Main Line]] [[Mizusawa-Esashi Station]].The artifacts uncovered in excavations are preserved at the Isawa Local History Museum.

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsunozuka Kofun | native_name = 角塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsunozuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsunozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Iwate Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsunozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|39|08|29|N|141|05|37|E||display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōshū, Iwate]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th or early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1935, 1945, 1947, 1974-75 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Tsunozuka Kofun'''|角塚古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the Isawa neighborhood of the city of [[Ōshū, Iwate]] in the [[Tōhoku region]] of northern [[Japan]]. It is significant in that it is the northernmost keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' yet discovered,<ref>{{Cite book|last=Mizoguchi|first=Koji|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=CZM2AQAAQBAJ|title=The Archaeology of Japan: From the Earliest Rice Farming Villages to the Rise of the State|date=|publisher=Cambridge University Press|year=2013|isbn=978-0-521-88490-7|location=|pages=242n|language=en|author-link=Koji Mizoguchi}}</ref> and indicates the penetration of [[Kofun period]] culture into lands traditionally considered part of the [[Emishi]] cultural zone.
==Overview== The Tsunozuka Kofun is located in the southern portion of the {{Interlanguage link|Kitakami Basin|ja|北上盆地}} of the [[Kitakami River]] on the [[alluvial fan]] of the [[Isawa River]]. The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name or rank of the person buried within is unknown. From the style of the mound and [[grave goods]] uncovered within, archaeologists have dated the ''kofun'' to the late 5th or early 6th century AD.
The ''kofun'' was excavated in 1935 and 1945, during which time a number of ''[[haniwa]]'' in the shapes of animals, people and buildings were discovered. However, it was not until 1947 that archaeologists confirmed that the structure was that of a {{nihongo|keyhole-shaped ''kofun''|前方後円墳|zenpō-kōen-fun}}. The site received protection as an Iwate Prefectural Historic Site in 1957. During further excavations held from 1974-1975, more ''haniwa'' and the ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones which originally covered the tomb were uncovered. The site received further protection as a National Historic Site in 1985.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp?register_id=401&item_id=168 |title=角塚古墳|trans-title=Tsunozuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
*Total length: 43–45 meters *Circular Portion: 2-tier, 28 meter diameter x 4 meter height *Keyway Portion: 10 meter width in the north, tapering out to 15 meters in the south, 1.5 meter height
The site is about 15 minutes by car from [[JR East]] [[Tōhoku Main Line]] [[Mizusawa-Esashi Station]].The artifacts uncovered in excavations are preserved at the Isawa Local History Museum.

[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Iwate Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ōshū, Iwate]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:kk]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsunozuka Kofun | native_name = 角塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsunozuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsunozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Iwate Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsunozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|39|08|29|N|141|05|37|E||display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōshū, Iwate]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th or early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1935, 1945, 1947, 1974-75 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Tsunozuka Kofun'''|角塚古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the Isawa neighborhood of the city of [[Ōshū, Iwate]] in the [[Tōhoku region]] of northern [[Japan]]. It is significant in that it is the northernmost keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' yet discovered,<ref>{{Cite book|last=Mizoguchi|first=Koji|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=CZM2AQAAQBAJ|title=The Archaeology of Japan: From the Earliest Rice Farming Villages to the Rise of the State|date=|publisher=Cambridge University Press|year=2013|isbn=978-0-521-88490-7|location=|pages=242n|language=en|author-link=Koji Mizoguchi}}</ref> and indicates the penetration of [[Kofun period]] culture into lands traditionally considered part of the [[Emishi]] cultural zone.
==Overview== The Tsunozuka Kofun is located in the southern portion of the {{Interlanguage link|Kitakami Basin|ja|北上盆地}} of the [[Kitakami River]] on the [[alluvial fan]] of the [[Isawa River]]. The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name or rank of the person buried within is unknown. From the style of the mound and [[grave goods]] uncovered within, archaeologists have dated the ''kofun'' to the late 5th or early 6th century AD.
The ''kofun'' was excavated in 1935 and 1945, during which time a number of ''[[haniwa]]'' in the shapes of animals, people and buildings were discovered. However, it was not until 1947 that archaeologists confirmed that the structure was that of a {{nihongo|keyhole-shaped ''kofun''|前方後円墳|zenpō-kōen-fun}}. The site received protection as an Iwate Prefectural Historic Site in 1957. During further excavations held from 1974-1975, more ''haniwa'' and the ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones which originally covered the tomb were uncovered. The site received further protection as a National Historic Site in 1985.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp?register_id=401&item_id=168 |title=角塚古墳|trans-title=Tsunozuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
*Total length: 43–45 meters *Circular Portion: 2-tier, 28 meter diameter x 4 meter height *Keyway Portion: 10 meter width in the north, tapering out to 15 meters in the south, 1.5 meter height
The site is about 15 minutes by car from [[JR East]] [[Tōhoku Main Line]] [[Mizusawa-Esashi Station]].The artifacts uncovered in excavations are preserved at the Isawa Local History Museum.
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Iwate)]]

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsunozuka Kofun | native_name = 角塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsunozuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsunozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Iwate Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsunozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|39|08|29|N|141|05|37|E||display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōshū, Iwate]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th or early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1935, 1945, 1947, 1974-75 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Tsunozuka Kofun'''|角塚古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the Isawa neighborhood of the city of [[Ōshū, Iwate]] in the [[Tōhoku region]] of northern [[Japan]]. It is significant in that it is the northernmost keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' yet discovered,<ref>{{Cite book|last=Mizoguchi|first=Koji|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=CZM2AQAAQBAJ|title=The Archaeology of Japan: From the Earliest Rice Farming Villages to the Rise of the State|date=|publisher=Cambridge University Press|year=2013|isbn=978-0-521-88490-7|location=|pages=242n|language=en|author-link=Koji Mizoguchi}}</ref> and indicates the penetration of [[Kofun period]] culture into lands traditionally considered part of the [[Emishi]] cultural zone.
==Overview== The Tsunozuka Kofun is located in the southern portion of the {{Interlanguage link|Kitakami Basin|ja|北上盆地}} of the [[Kitakami River]] on the [[alluvial fan]] of the [[Isawa River]]. The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name or rank of the person buried within is unknown. From the style of the mound and [[grave goods]] uncovered within, archaeologists have dated the ''kofun'' to the late 5th or early 6th century AD.
The ''kofun'' was excavated in 1935 and 1945, during which time a number of ''[[haniwa]]'' in the shapes of animals, people and buildings were discovered. However, it was not until 1947 that archaeologists confirmed that the structure was that of a {{nihongo|keyhole-shaped ''kofun''|前方後円墳|zenpō-kōen-fun}}. The site received protection as an Iwate Prefectural Historic Site in 1957. During further excavations held from 1974-1975, more ''haniwa'' and the ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones which originally covered the tomb were uncovered. The site received further protection as a National Historic Site in 1985.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp?register_id=401&item_id=168 |title=角塚古墳|trans-title=Tsunozuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
*Total length: 43–45 meters *Circular Portion: 2-tier, 28 meter diameter x 4 meter height *Keyway Portion: 10 meter width in the north, tapering out to 15 meters in the south, 1.5 meter height
The site is about 15 minutes by car from [[JR East]] [[Tōhoku Main Line]] [[Mizusawa-Esashi Station]].The artifacts uncovered in excavations are preserved at the Isawa Local History Museum.
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Iwate)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Tsunozuka-kofun}} * [http://www.city.oshu.iwate.jp/kanko/view.rbz?nd=401&ik=1&pnp=397&pnp=401&cd=1567 Oshu City Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215759 Cultural Heritage Online] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsunozuka Kofun | native_name = 角塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsunozuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsunozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Iwate Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsunozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|39|08|29|N|141|05|37|E||display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōshū, Iwate]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th or early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1935, 1945, 1947, 1974-75 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Tsunozuka Kofun'''|角塚古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the Isawa neighborhood of the city of [[Ōshū, Iwate]] in the [[Tōhoku region]] of northern [[Japan]]. It is significant in that it is the northernmost keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' yet discovered,<ref>{{Cite book|last=Mizoguchi|first=Koji|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=CZM2AQAAQBAJ|title=The Archaeology of Japan: From the Earliest Rice Farming Villages to the Rise of the State|date=|publisher=Cambridge University Press|year=2013|isbn=978-0-521-88490-7|location=|pages=242n|language=en|author-link=Koji Mizoguchi}}</ref> and indicates the penetration of [[Kofun period]] culture into lands traditionally considered part of the [[Emishi]] cultural zone.
==Overview== The Tsunozuka Kofun is located in the southern portion of the {{Interlanguage link|Kitakami Basin|ja|北上盆地}} of the [[Kitakami River]] on the [[alluvial fan]] of the [[Isawa River]]. The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name or rank of the person buried within is unknown. From the style of the mound and [[grave goods]] uncovered within, archaeologists have dated the ''kofun'' to the late 5th or early 6th century AD.
The ''kofun'' was excavated in 1935 and 1945, during which time a number of ''[[haniwa]]'' in the shapes of animals, people and buildings were discovered. However, it was not until 1947 that archaeologists confirmed that the structure was that of a {{nihongo|keyhole-shaped ''kofun''|前方後円墳|zenpō-kōen-fun}}. The site received protection as an Iwate Prefectural Historic Site in 1957. During further excavations held from 1974-1975, more ''haniwa'' and the ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones which originally covered the tomb were uncovered. The site received further protection as a National Historic Site in 1985.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp?register_id=401&item_id=168 |title=角塚古墳|trans-title=Tsunozuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
*Total length: 43–45 meters *Circular Portion: 2-tier, 28 meter diameter x 4 meter height *Keyway Portion: 10 meter width in the north, tapering out to 15 meters in the south, 1.5 meter height
The site is about 15 minutes by car from [[JR East]] [[Tōhoku Main Line]] [[Mizusawa-Esashi Station]].The artifacts uncovered in excavations are preserved at the Isawa Local History Museum.
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Iwate)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Tsunozuka-kofun}} * [http://www.city.oshu.iwate.jp/kanko/view.rbz?nd=401&ik=1&pnp=397&pnp=401&cd=1567 Oshu City Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215759 Cultural Heritage Online] {{in lang|ja}}

[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Iwate Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ōshū, Iwate]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:kk]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsunozuka Kofun | native_name = 角塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsunozuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsunozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Iwate Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsunozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|39|08|29|N|141|05|37|E||display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōshū, Iwate]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th or early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1935, 1945, 1947, 1974-75 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Tsunozuka Kofun'''|角塚古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Isawa neighborhood of the city of [[Ōshū, Iwate]] in the [[Tōhoku region]] of northern [[Japan]]. It is significant in that it is the northernmost keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' yet discovered,<ref>{{Cite book|last=Mizoguchi|first=Koji|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=CZM2AQAAQBAJ|title=The Archaeology of Japan: From the Earliest Rice Farming Villages to the Rise of the State|date=|publisher=Cambridge University Press|year=2013|isbn=978-0-521-88490-7|location=|pages=242n|language=en|author-link=Koji Mizoguchi}}</ref> and indicates the penetration of [[Kofun period]] culture into lands traditionally considered part of the [[Emishi]] cultural zone.
==Overview== The Tsunozuka Kofun is located in the southern portion of the {{Interlanguage link|Kitakami Basin|ja|北上盆地}} of the [[Kitakami River]] on the [[alluvial fan]] of the [[Isawa River]]. The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name or rank of the person buried within is unknown. From the style of the mound and [[grave goods]] uncovered within, archaeologists have dated the ''kofun'' to the late 5th or early 6th century AD.
The ''kofun'' was excavated in 1935 and 1945, during which time a number of ''[[haniwa]]'' in the shapes of animals, people and buildings were discovered. However, it was not until 1947 that archaeologists confirmed that the structure was that of a {{nihongo|keyhole-shaped ''kofun''|前方後円墳|zenpō-kōen-fun}}. The site received protection as an Iwate Prefectural Historic Site in 1957. During further excavations held from 1974-1975, more ''haniwa'' and the ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones which originally covered the tomb were uncovered. The site received further protection as a National Historic Site in 1985.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp?register_id=401&item_id=168 |title=角塚古墳|trans-title=Tsunozuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
*Total length: 43–45 meters *Circular Portion: 2-tier, 28 meter diameter x 4 meter height *Keyway Portion: 10 meter width in the north, tapering out to 15 meters in the south, 1.5 meter height
The site is about 15 minutes by car from [[JR East]] [[Tōhoku Main Line]] [[Mizusawa-Esashi Station]].The artifacts uncovered in excavations are preserved at the Isawa Local History Museum.
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Iwate)]]

However, it was not until 1947 that archaeologists confirmed that the structure was that of a {{nihongo|keyhole-shaped ''kofun''|前方後円墳|zenpō-kōen-fun}}. The site received protection as an Iwate Prefectural Historic Site in 1957. During further excavations held from 1974-1975, more ''haniwa'' and the ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones which originally covered the tomb were uncovered. The site received further protection as a National Historic Site in 1985.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp?register_id=401&item_id=168 |title=角塚古墳|trans-title=Tsunozuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
*Total length: 43–45 meters *Circular Portion: 2-tier, 28 meter diameter x 4 meter height *Keyway Portion: 10 meter width in the north, tapering out to 15 meters in the south, 1.5 meter height
The site is about 15 minutes by car from [[JR East]] [[Tōhoku Main Line]] [[Mizusawa-Esashi Station]].The artifacts uncovered in excavations are preserved at the Isawa Local History Museum.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tsunozuka Kofun | native_name = 角塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsunozuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsunozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Iwate Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsunozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|39|08|29|N|141|05|37|E||display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōshū, Iwate]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th or early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1935, 1945, 1947, 1974-75 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Tsunozuka Kofun'''|角塚古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Isawa neighborhood of the city of [[Ōshū, Iwate]] in the [[Tōhoku region]] of northern [[Japan]]. It is significant in that it is the northernmost keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' yet discovered,<ref>{{Cite book|last=Mizoguchi|first=Koji|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=CZM2AQAAQBAJ|title=The Archaeology of Japan: From the Earliest Rice Farming Villages to the Rise of the State|date=|publisher=Cambridge University Press|year=2013|isbn=978-0-521-88490-7|location=|pages=242n|language=en|author-link=Koji Mizoguchi}}</ref> and indicates the penetration of [[Kofun period]] culture into lands traditionally considered part of the [[Emishi]] cultural zone.
==Overview== The Tsunozuka Kofun is located in the southern portion of the {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kitakami Basin|ja|北上盆地}} of the [[Kitakami River]] on the [[alluvial fan]] of the [[Isawa River]]. The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name or rank of the person buried within is unknown. From the style of the mound and [[grave goods]] uncovered within, archaeologists have dated the ''kofun'' to the late 5th or early 6th century AD.
The ''kofun'' was excavated in 1935 and 1945, during which time a number of ''[[haniwa]]'' in the shapes of animals, people and buildings were discovered. However, it was not until 1947 that archaeologists confirmed that the structure was that of a {{nihongo|keyhole-shaped ''kofun''|前方後円墳|zenpō-kōen-fun}}. The site received protection as an Iwate Prefectural Historic Site in 1957. During further excavations held from 1974-1975, more ''haniwa'' and the ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones which originally covered the tomb were uncovered. The site received further protection as a National Historic Site in 1985.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp?register_id=401&item_id=168 |title=角塚古墳|trans-title=Tsunozuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
*Total length: 43–45 meters *Circular Portion: 2-tier, 28 meter diameter x 4 meter height *Keyway Portion: 10 meter width in the north, tapering out to 15 meters in the south, 1.5 meter height
The site is about 15 minutes by car from [[JR East]] [[Tōhoku Main Line]] [[Mizusawa-Esashi Station]].The artifacts uncovered in excavations are preserved at the Isawa Local History Museum.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tsunozuka Kofun | native_name = 角塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsunozuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsunozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Iwate Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsunozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|39|08|29|N|141|05|37|E||display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōshū, Iwate]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th or early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1935, 1945, 1947, 1974-75 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Tsunozuka Kofun'''|角塚古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Isawa neighborhood of the city of [[Ōshū, Iwate]] in the [[Tōhoku region]] of northern [[Japan]]. It is significant in that it is the northernmost keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' yet discovered,<ref>{{Cite book|last=Mizoguchi|first=Koji|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=CZM2AQAAQBAJ|title=The Archaeology of Japan: From the Earliest Rice Farming Villages to the Rise of the State|date=|publisher=Cambridge University Press|year=2013|isbn=978-0-521-88490-7|location=|pages=242n|language=en|author-link=Koji Mizoguchi}}</ref> and indicates the penetration of [[Kofun period]] culture into lands traditionally considered part of the [[Emishi]] cultural zone.
==Overview== The Tsunozuka Kofun is located in the southern portion of the {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kitakami Basin|ja|北上盆地}} of the [[Kitakami River]] on the [[alluvial fan]] of the [[Isawa River]]. The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name or rank of the person buried within is unknown. From the style of the mound and [[grave goods]] uncovered within, archaeologists have dated the ''kofun'' to the late 5th or early 6th century AD.
The ''kofun'' was excavated in 1935 and 1945, during which time a number of ''[[haniwa]]'' in the shapes of animals, people and buildings were discovered. However, it was not until 1947 that archaeologists confirmed that the structure was that of a {{nihongo|keyhole-shaped ''kofun''|前方後円墳|zenpō-kōen-fun}}. The site received protection as an Iwate Prefectural Historic Site in 1957. During further excavations held from 1974-1975, more ''haniwa'' and the ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones which originally covered the tomb were uncovered. The site received further protection as a National Historic Site in 1985.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp?register_id=401&item_id=168 |title=角塚古墳|trans-title=Tsunozuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
*Total length: 43–45 meters *Circular Portion: 2-tier, 28 meter diameter x 4 meter height *Keyway Portion: 10 meter width in the north, tapering out to 15 meters in the south, 1.5 meter height
The site is about 15 minutes by car from [[JR East]] [[Tōhoku Main Line]] [[Mizusawa-Esashi Station]].The artifacts uncovered in excavations are preserved at the Isawa Local History Museum.
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Iwate)]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tsunozuka Kofun | native_name = 角塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsunozuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsunozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Iwate Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsunozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|39|08|29|N|141|05|37|E||display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōshū, Iwate]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th or early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1935, 1945, 1947, 1974-75 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Tsunozuka Kofun'''|角塚古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Isawa neighborhood of the city of [[Ōshū, Iwate]] in the [[Tōhoku region]] of northern [[Japan]]. It is significant in that it is the northernmost keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' yet discovered,<ref>{{Cite book|last=Mizoguchi|first=Koji|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=CZM2AQAAQBAJ|title=The Archaeology of Japan: From the Earliest Rice Farming Villages to the Rise of the State|date=|publisher=Cambridge University Press|year=2013|isbn=978-0-521-88490-7|location=|pages=242n|language=en|author-link=Koji Mizoguchi}}</ref> and indicates the penetration of [[Kofun period]] culture into lands traditionally considered part of the [[Emishi]] cultural zone.
==Overview== The Tsunozuka Kofun is located in the southern portion of the {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kitakami Basin|ja|北上盆地}} of the [[Kitakami River]] on the [[alluvial fan]] of the [[Isawa River]]. The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name or rank of the person buried within is unknown. From the style of the mound and [[grave goods]] uncovered within, archaeologists have dated the ''kofun'' to the late 5th or early 6th century AD.
The ''kofun'' was excavated in 1935 and 1945, during which time a number of ''[[haniwa]]'' in the shapes of animals, people and buildings were discovered. However, it was not until 1947 that archaeologists confirmed that the structure was that of a {{nihongo|keyhole-shaped ''kofun''|前方後円墳|zenpō-kōen-fun}}. The site received protection as an Iwate Prefectural Historic Site in 1957. During further excavations held from 1974-1975, more ''haniwa'' and the ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones which originally covered the tomb were uncovered. The site received further protection as a National Historic Site in 1985.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp?register_id=401&item_id=168 |title=角塚古墳|trans-title=Tsunozuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
*Total length: 43–45 meters *Circular Portion: 2-tier, 28 meter diameter x 4 meter height *Keyway Portion: 10 meter width in the north, tapering out to 15 meters in the south, 1.5 meter height
The site is about 15 minutes by car from [[JR East]] [[Tōhoku Main Line]] [[Mizusawa-Esashi Station]].The artifacts uncovered in excavations are preserved at the Isawa Local History Museum.
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Iwate)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Tsunozuka-kofun}} * [http://www.city.oshu.iwate.jp/kanko/view.rbz?nd=401&ik=1&pnp=397&pnp=401&cd=1567 Oshu City Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215759 Cultural Heritage Online] {{in lang|ja}}

*Total length: 43–45 meters *Circular Portion: 2-tier, 28 meter diameter x 4 meter height *Keyway Portion: 10 meter width in the north, tapering out to 15 meters in the south, 1.5 meter height

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tsunozuka Kofun | native_name = 角塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsunozuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsunozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Iwate Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsunozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|39|08|29|N|141|05|37|E||display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōshū, Iwate]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th or early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1935, 1945, 1947, 1974-75 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Tsunozuka Kofun'''|角塚古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Isawa neighborhood of the city of [[Ōshū, Iwate]] in the [[Tōhoku region]] of northern [[Japan]]. It is significant in that it is the northernmost keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' yet discovered,<ref>{{Cite book|last=Mizoguchi|first=Koji|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=CZM2AQAAQBAJ|title=The Archaeology of Japan: From the Earliest Rice Farming Villages to the Rise of the State|date=|publisher=Cambridge University Press|year=2013|isbn=978-0-521-88490-7|location=|pages=242n|language=en|author-link=Koji Mizoguchi}}</ref> and indicates the penetration of [[Kofun period]] culture into lands traditionally considered part of the [[Emishi]] cultural zone.
==Overview== The Tsunozuka Kofun is located in the southern portion of the {{ill|Kitakami Basin|ja|北上盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakami Basin|qq=}} of the [[Kitakami River]] on the [[alluvial fan]] of the [[Isawa River]]. The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name or Rank of the person buried within is unknown. From the style of the mound and [[grave goods]] uncovered within, archaeologists have dated the ''kofun'' to the late 5th or early 6th century AD.
The ''kofun'' was excavated in 1935 and 1945, during which time a number of ''[[haniwa]]'' in the shapes of animals, people and buildings were discovered. However, it was not until 1947 that archaeologists confirmed that the structure was that of a {{nihongo|keyhole-shaped ''kofun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun}}. The site received protection as an Iwate Prefectural Historic Site in 1957. During further excavations held from 1974-1975, more ''haniwa'' and the ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones which originally covered the tomb were uncovered. The site received further protection as a National Historic Site in 1985.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp?register_id=401&item_id=168 |title=角塚古墳|trans-title=Tsunozuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
*Total length: 43–45 meters *Circular Portion: 2-tier, 28 meter diameter x 4 meter height *Keyway Portion: 10 meter width in the north, tapering out to 15 meters in the south, 1.5 meter height
The site is about 15 minutes by car from [[JR East]] [[Tōhoku Main Line]] [[Mizusawa-Esashi Station]].The artifacts uncovered in excavations are preserved at the Isawa Local History Museum.
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Iwate)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Tsunozuka-kofun}} * [http://www.city.oshu.iwate.jp/kanko/view.rbz?nd=401&ik=1&pnp=397&pnp=401&cd=1567 Oshu City Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215759 Cultural Heritage Online] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

[[Category:Completion tier 5]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Fixed Kofun templates
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tsunozuka Kofun | native_name = 角塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsunozuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsunozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Iwate Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsunozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|39|08|29|N|141|05|37|E||display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōshū, Iwate]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th or early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1935, 1945, 1947, 1974-75 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Tsunozuka Kofun'''|角塚古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Isawa neighborhood of the city of [[Ōshū, Iwate]] in the [[Tōhoku region]] of northern [[Japan]]. It is significant in that it is the northernmost keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' yet discovered,<ref>{{Cite book|last=Mizoguchi|first=Koji|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=CZM2AQAAQBAJ|title=The Archaeology of Japan: From the Earliest Rice Farming Villages to the Rise of the State|date=|publisher=Cambridge University Press|year=2013|isbn=978-0-521-88490-7|location=|pages=242n|language=en|author-link=Koji Mizoguchi}}</ref> and indicates the penetration of [[Kofun period]] culture into lands traditionally considered part of the [[Emishi]] cultural zone.
==Overview== The Tsunozuka Kofun is located in the southern portion of the {{ill|Kitakami Basin|ja|北上盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakami Basin|qq=}} of the [[Kitakami River]] on the [[alluvial fan]] of the [[Isawa River]]. The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name or Rank of the person buried within is unknown. From the style of the mound and [[grave goods]] uncovered within, archaeologists have dated the ''kofun'' to the late 5th or early 6th century AD.
The ''kofun'' was excavated in 1935 and 1945, during which time a number of ''[[haniwa]]'' in the shapes of animals, people and buildings were discovered. However, it was not until 1947 that archaeologists confirmed that the structure was that of a {{nihongo|keyhole-shaped ''kofun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun}}. The site received protection as an Iwate Prefectural Historic Site in 1957. During further excavations held from 1974-1975, more ''haniwa'' and the ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones which originally covered the tomb were uncovered. The site received further protection as a National Historic Site in 1985.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp?register_id=401&item_id=168 |title=角塚古墳|trans-title=Tsunozuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
*Total length: 43–45 meters *Circular Portion: 2-tier, 28 meter diameter x 4 meter height *Keyway Portion: 10 meter width in the north, tapering out to 15 meters in the south, 1.5 meter height
The site is about 15 minutes by car from [[JR East]] [[Tōhoku Main Line]] [[Mizusawa-Esashi Station]].The artifacts uncovered in excavations are preserved at the Isawa Local History Museum.
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Iwate)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Tsunozuka-kofun}} * [http://www.city.oshu.iwate.jp/kanko/view.rbz?nd=401&ik=1&pnp=397&pnp=401&cd=1567 Oshu City Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215759 Cultural Heritage Online] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

The site is about 15 minutes by car from [[JR East]] [[Tōhoku Main Line]] [[Mizusawa-Esashi Station]].The artifacts uncovered in excavations are preserved at the Isawa Local History Museum.

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tsunozuka Kofun | native_name = 角塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsunozuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsunozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Iwate Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsunozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|39|08|29|N|141|05|37|E||display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Ōshū, Iwate|Ōshū, Iwate]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th or early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1935, 1945, 1947, 1974-75 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Tsunozuka Kofun'''|角塚古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Isawa neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Ōshū, Iwate|Ōshū, Iwate]] in the [[Tōhoku region]] of northern [[Japan]]. It is significant in that it is the northernmost keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' yet discovered,<ref>{{Cite book|last=Mizoguchi|first=Koji|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=CZM2AQAAQBAJ|title=The Archaeology of Japan: From the Earliest Rice Farming Villages to the Rise of the State|date=|publisher=Cambridge University Press|year=2013|isbn=978-0-521-88490-7|location=|pages=242n|language=en|author-link=Koji Mizoguchi}}</ref> and indicates the penetration of [[Kofun period]] culture into lands traditionally considered part of the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]] cultural zone.
==Overview== The Tsunozuka Kofun is located in the southern portion of the {{ill|Kitakami Basin|ja|北上盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakami Basin|qq=}} of the [[Kitakami River]] on the [[:en:Alluvial fan|Alluvial fan]] of the [[Isawa River]]. The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name or Rank of the person buried within is unknown. From the style of the mound and [[grave goods]] uncovered within, archaeologists have dated the ''kofun'' to the late 5th or early 6th century AD.
The ''kofun'' was excavated in 1935 and 1945, during which time a number of ''[[haniwa]]'' in the shapes of animals, people and buildings were discovered. However, it was not until 1947 that archaeologists confirmed that the structure was that of a {{nihongo|keyhole-shaped ''kofun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun}}. The site received protection as an Iwate Prefectural Historic Site in 1957. During further excavations held from 1974-1975, more ''haniwa'' and the ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones which originally covered the tomb were uncovered. The site received further protection as a National Historic Site in 1985.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp?register_id=401&item_id=168 |title=角塚古墳|trans-title=Tsunozuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
*Total length: 43–45 meters *Circular Portion: 2-tier, 28 meter diameter x 4 meter height *Keyway Portion: 10 meter width in the north, tapering out to 15 meters in the south, 1.5 meter height
The site is about 15 minutes by car from [[JR East]] [[Tōhoku Main Line]] [[Mizusawa-Esashi Station]].The artifacts uncovered in excavations are preserved at the Isawa Local History Museum.
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Iwate)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Tsunozuka-kofun}} * [http://www.city.oshu.iwate.jp/kanko/view.rbz?nd=401&ik=1&pnp=397&pnp=401&cd=1567 Oshu City Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215759 Cultural Heritage Online] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

It is significant in that it is the northernmost keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' yet discovered,<ref>{{Cite book|last=Mizoguchi|first=Koji|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=CZM2AQAAQBAJ|title=The Archaeology of Japan: From the Earliest Rice Farming Villages to the Rise of the State|date=|publisher=Cambridge University Press|year=2013|isbn=978-0-521-88490-7|location=|pages=242n|language=en|author-link=Koji Mizoguchi}}</ref> and indicates the penetration of [[Kofun period]] culture into lands traditionally considered part of the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]] cultural zone.
==Overview== The Tsunozuka Kofun is located in the southern portion of the {{ill|Kitakami Basin|ja|北上盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakami Basin|qq=}} of the [[Kitakami River]] on the [[:en:Alluvial fan|Alluvial fan]] of the [[Isawa River]]. The ''kofun'' was excavated in 1935 and 1945, during which time a number of ''[[haniwa]]'' in the shapes of animals, people and buildings were discovered. However, it was not until 1947 that archaeologists confirmed that the structure was that of a {{nihongo|keyhole-shaped ''kofun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun}}. The site received protection as an Iwate Prefectural Historic Site in 1957. During further excavations held from 1974-1975, more ''haniwa'' and the ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones which originally covered the tomb were uncovered. The site received further protection as a National Historic Site in 1985.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp?register_id=401&item_id=168 |title=角塚古墳|trans-title=Tsunozuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
*Total length: 43–45 meters *Circular Portion: 2-tier, 28 meter diameter x 4 meter height *Keyway Portion: 10 meter width in the north, tapering out to 15 meters in the south, 1.5 meter height
The site is about 15 minutes by car from [[JR East]] [[Tōhoku Main Line]] [[Mizusawa-Esashi Station]].The artifacts uncovered in excavations are preserved at the Isawa Local History Museum.
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Iwate)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Tsunozuka-kofun}} * [http://www.city.oshu.iwate.jp/kanko/view.rbz?nd=401&ik=1&pnp=397&pnp=401&cd=1567 Oshu City Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215759 Cultural Heritage Online] {{in lang|ja}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tsunozuka Kofun | native_name = 角塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsunozuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsunozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Iwate Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsunozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|39|08|29|N|141|05|37|E||display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Ōshū, Iwate|Ōshū, Iwate]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th or early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1935, 1945, 1947, 1974-75 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Tsunozuka Kofun'''|角塚古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Isawa neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Ōshū, Iwate|Ōshū, Iwate]] in the [[Tōhoku region]] of northern [[Japan]]. It is significant in that it is the northernmost keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' yet discovered,<ref>{{Cite book|last=Mizoguchi|first=Koji|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=CZM2AQAAQBAJ|title=The Archaeology of Japan: From the Earliest Rice Farming Villages to the Rise of the State|date=|publisher=Cambridge University Press|year=2013|isbn=978-0-521-88490-7|location=|pages=242n|language=en|author-link=Koji Mizoguchi}}</ref> and indicates the penetration of [[Kofun period]] culture into lands traditionally considered part of the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]] cultural zone.
==Overview== The Tsunozuka Kofun is located in the southern portion of the {{ill|Kitakami Basin|ja|北上盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakami Basin|qq=}} of the [[Kitakami River]] on the [[:en:Alluvial fan|Alluvial fan]] of the [[:en:Isawa River|Isawa River]]. The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name or Rank of the person buried within is unknown. From the style of the mound and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] uncovered within, archaeologists have dated the ''kofun'' to the late 5th or early 6th century AD.
The ''kofun'' was excavated in 1935 and 1945, during which time a number of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' in the shapes of animals, people and buildings were discovered. However, it was not until 1947 that archaeologists confirmed that the structure was that of a {{nihongo|keyhole-shaped ''kofun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun}}. The site received protection as an Iwate Prefectural Historic Site in 1957. During further excavations held from 1974-1975, more ''haniwa'' and the ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones which originally covered the tomb were uncovered. The site received further protection as a National Historic Site in 1985.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp?register_id=401&item_id=168 |title=角塚古墳|trans-title=Tsunozuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
*Total length: 43–45 meters *Circular Portion: 2-tier, 28 meter diameter x 4 meter height *Keyway Portion: 10 meter width in the north, tapering out to 15 meters in the south, 1.5 meter height
The site is about 15 minutes by car from [[JR East]] [[Tōhoku Main Line]] [[Mizusawa-Esashi Station]].The artifacts uncovered in excavations are preserved at the Isawa Local History Museum.
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Iwate)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Tsunozuka-kofun}} * [http://www.city.oshu.iwate.jp/kanko/view.rbz?nd=401&ik=1&pnp=397&pnp=401&cd=1567 Oshu City Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215759 Cultural Heritage Online] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

[[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]]
[[Category:Completion tier 5]]
[[sn:Tsunozuka Kofun]]}}

The site is about 15 minutes by car from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[Tōhoku Main Line]] [[Mizusawa-Esashi Station]].The artifacts uncovered in excavations are preserved at the Isawa Local History Museum.

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tsunozuka Kofun | native_name = 角塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsunozuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsunozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Iwate Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsunozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|39|08|29|N|141|05|37|E||display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Ōshū, Iwate|Ōshū, Iwate]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th or early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1935, 1945, 1947, 1974-75 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Tsunozuka Kofun'''|角塚古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Isawa neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Ōshū, Iwate|Ōshū, Iwate]] in the [[Tōhoku region]] of northern [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is significant in that it is the northernmost keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' yet discovered,<ref>{{Cite book|last=Mizoguchi|first=Koji|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=CZM2AQAAQBAJ|title=The Archaeology of Japan: From the Earliest Rice Farming Villages to the Rise of the State|date=|publisher=Cambridge University Press|year=2013|isbn=978-0-521-88490-7|location=|pages=242n|language=en|author-link=Koji Mizoguchi}}</ref> and indicates the penetration of [[Kofun period]] culture into lands traditionally considered part of the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]] cultural zone.
==Overview== The Tsunozuka Kofun is located in the southern portion of the {{ill|Kitakami Basin|ja|北上盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakami Basin|qq=}} of the [[:en:Kitakami River|Kitakami River]] on the [[:en:Alluvial fan|Alluvial fan]] of the [[:en:Isawa River|Isawa River]]. The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name or Rank of the person buried within is unknown. From the style of the mound and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] uncovered within, archaeologists have dated the ''kofun'' to the late 5th or early 6th century AD.
The ''kofun'' was excavated in 1935 and 1945, during which time a number of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' in the shapes of animals, people and buildings were discovered. However, it was not until 1947 that archaeologists confirmed that the structure was that of a {{nihongo|keyhole-shaped ''kofun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun}}. The site received protection as an Iwate Prefectural Historic Site in 1957. During further excavations held from 1974-1975, more ''haniwa'' and the ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones which originally covered the tomb were uncovered. The site received further protection as a National Historic Site in 1985.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp?register_id=401&item_id=168 |title=角塚古墳|trans-title=Tsunozuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
*Total length: 43–45 meters *Circular Portion: 2-tier, 28 meter diameter x 4 meter height *Keyway Portion: 10 meter width in the north, tapering out to 15 meters in the south, 1.5 meter height
The site is about 15 minutes by car from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[Tōhoku Main Line]] [[Mizusawa-Esashi Station]].The artifacts uncovered in excavations are preserved at the Isawa Local History Museum.
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Iwate)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Iwate)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Tsunozuka-kofun}} * [http://www.city.oshu.iwate.jp/kanko/view.rbz?nd=401&ik=1&pnp=397&pnp=401&cd=1567 Oshu City Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215759 Cultural Heritage Online] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

[[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]]
[[Category:Completion tier 5]]
[[sn:Tsunozuka Kofun]]}}

However, it was not until 1947 that archaeologists confirmed that the structure was that of a {{nihongo|keyhole-shaped ''kofun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun}}. The site is about 15 minutes by car from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[Tōhoku Main Line]] [[:en:Mizusawa-Esashi Station|Mizusawa-Esashi Station]].The artifacts uncovered in excavations are preserved at the Isawa Local History Museum.

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11630689]] Wikidata name: Tsunozuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tsunozuka Kofun | native_name = 角塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Tsunozuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsunozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Iwate Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsunozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|39.141506|N|141.09375|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Oshu|qid=Q386052}} | region = [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th or early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1935, 1945, 1947, 1974-75 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Tsunozuka Kofun'''|角塚古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Isawa neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Ōshū, Iwate|Ōshū, Iwate]] in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] of northern [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is significant in that it is the northernmost keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' yet discovered,<ref>{{Cite book|last=Mizoguchi|first=Koji|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=CZM2AQAAQBAJ|title=The Archaeology of Japan: From the Earliest Rice Farming Villages to the Rise of the State|date=|publisher=Cambridge University Press|year=2013|isbn=978-0-521-88490-7|location=|pages=242n|language=en|author-link=Koji Mizoguchi}}</ref> and indicates the penetration of [[Kofun period]] culture into lands traditionally considered part of the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]] cultural zone.
==Overview== The Tsunozuka Kofun is located in the southern portion of the {{ill|Kitakami Basin|ja|北上盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakami Basin|qq=}} of the [[:en:Kitakami River|Kitakami River]] on the [[:en:Alluvial fan|Alluvial fan]] of the [[:en:Isawa River|Isawa River]]. The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name or Rank of the person buried within is unknown. From the style of the mound and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] uncovered within, archaeologists have dated the ''kofun'' to the late 5th or early 6th century AD.
The ''kofun'' was excavated in 1935 and 1945, during which time a number of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' in the shapes of animals, people and buildings were discovered. However, it was not until 1947 that archaeologists confirmed that the structure was that of a {{nihongo|keyhole-shaped ''kofun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun}}. The site received protection as an Iwate Prefectural Historic Site in 1957. During further excavations held from 1974-1975, more ''haniwa'' and the ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones which originally covered the tomb were uncovered. The site received further protection as a National Historic Site in 1985.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp?register_id=401&item_id=168 |title=角塚古墳|trans-title=Tsunozuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
*Total length: 43–45 meters *Circular Portion: 2-tier, 28 meter diameter x 4 meter height *Keyway Portion: 10 meter width in the north, tapering out to 15 meters in the south, 1.5 meter height
The site is about 15 minutes by car from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]] [[:en:Mizusawa-Esashi Station|Mizusawa-Esashi Station]].The artifacts uncovered in excavations are preserved at the Isawa Local History Museum.
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Iwate)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Iwate)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Tsunozuka-kofun}} * [http://www.city.oshu.iwate.jp/kanko/view.rbz?nd=401&ik=1&pnp=397&pnp=401&cd=1567 Oshu City Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215759 Cultural Heritage Online] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tsunozuka-kofun]][[en:Tsunozuka Kofun]][[ja:角塚古墳]][[zh:角塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11630689]] Wikidata name: Tsunozuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tsunozuka Kofun | native_name = 角塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Tsunozuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsunozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Iwate Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsunozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|39.141506|N|141.09375|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Oshu|qid=Q386052}} | region = [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th or early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1935, 1945, 1947, 1974-75 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Tsunozuka Kofun'''|角塚古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Isawa neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Ōshū, Iwate|Ōshū, Iwate]] in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] of northern [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is significant in that it is the northernmost keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' yet discovered,<ref>{{Cite book|last=Mizoguchi|first=Koji|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=CZM2AQAAQBAJ|title=The Archaeology of Japan: From the Earliest Rice Farming Villages to the Rise of the State|date=|publisher=Cambridge University Press|year=2013|isbn=978-0-521-88490-7|location=|pages=242n|language=en|author-link=Koji Mizoguchi}}</ref> and indicates the penetration of [[Kofun period]] culture into lands traditionally considered part of the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]] cultural zone.
==Overview== The Tsunozuka Kofun is located in the southern portion of the {{ill|Kitakami Basin|ja|北上盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakami Basin|qq=|1=Kitakami Basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400868}} of the [[:en:Kitakami River|Kitakami River]] on the [[:en:Alluvial fan|Alluvial fan]] of the [[:en:Isawa River|Isawa River]]. The tomb does not appear in any historical records and the name or Rank of the person buried within is unknown. From the style of the mound and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] uncovered within, archaeologists have dated the ''kofun'' to the late 5th or early 6th century AD.
The ''kofun'' was excavated in 1935 and 1945, during which time a number of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' in the shapes of animals, people and buildings were discovered. However, it was not until 1947 that archaeologists confirmed that the structure was that of a {{nihongo|keyhole-shaped ''kofun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun}}. The site received protection as an Iwate Prefectural Historic Site in 1957. During further excavations held from 1974-1975, more ''haniwa'' and the ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones which originally covered the tomb were uncovered. The site received further protection as a National Historic Site in 1985.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp?register_id=401&item_id=168 |title=角塚古墳|trans-title=Tsunozuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
*Total length: 43–45 meters *Circular Portion: 2-tier, 28 meter diameter x 4 meter height *Keyway Portion: 10 meter width in the north, tapering out to 15 meters in the south, 1.5 meter height
The site is about 15 minutes by car from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]] [[:en:Mizusawa-Esashi Station|Mizusawa-Esashi Station]].The artifacts uncovered in excavations are preserved at the Isawa Local History Museum.
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Iwate)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Iwate)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Tsunozuka-kofun}} * [http://www.city.oshu.iwate.jp/kanko/view.rbz?nd=401&ik=1&pnp=397&pnp=401&cd=1567 Oshu City Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215759 Cultural Heritage Online] {{in lang|ja}}
